title: Pipeline Audit & Recommendations
description: Audit a mock sales pipeline of 25 deals, assess the health of each deal using MEDDIC, identify risks, calculate sales velocity, and present actionable recommendations to improve pipeline performance.
whyItMatters: Pipeline reviews are a weekly ritual at every sales organisation. Your manager will review your pipeline regularly and expect you to know: which deals are real, which are at risk, where the gaps are, and what you're doing about it. Being able to critically assess pipeline health — your own or a team's — is a fundamental operational skill.
skillsLearned: Pipeline assessment and deal health scoring
MEDDIC qualification applied to real deals

steps.title: Review the Mock Pipeline
steps.description: You'll work with a mock pipeline of 25 deals at various stages. For each deal, review: deal name, value, stage, days in stage, last activity date, number of stakeholders engaged, competition status, and next steps. Create a summary spreadsheet.
steps.tips: Create your own mock data if needed. Include a mix: some healthy deals progressing well, some stalled deals with no recent activity, some deals with missing information, and some deals that should be disqualified.

steps.title: Score Each Deal with MEDDIC
steps.description: For each deal, assess how well it scores on MEDDIC criteria. Use a simple RAG (Red/Amber/Green) rating for each element: Metrics, Economic Buyer, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Identify Pain, Champion. Flag any deal where more than 2 elements are red.
steps.tips: A deal without an identified champion is almost certainly going to stall. A deal without economic buyer access will die in procurement. These are the two most common fatal gaps.

steps.title: Calculate Sales Velocity
steps.description: Using the pipeline data, calculate overall sales velocity: (Number of Opportunities x Average Deal Value x Win Rate) / Average Cycle Length. Then calculate velocity for each pipeline stage to identify where deals slow down or drop off.
steps.tips: If deals are spending more than 2x the average time in a stage, they're probably stalled. Create a 'stalled deals' list and recommend actions for each.

steps.title: Identify Pipeline Gaps
steps.description: Assess overall pipeline health: Is there enough pipeline coverage (target: 3-4x quota)? Is the stage distribution healthy (not too top-heavy or bottom-heavy)? Are there enough new opportunities entering the pipe? What's the average age of deals?
steps.tips: A healthy pipeline looks like a funnel — wider at the top, narrower at the bottom. If most of your pipeline is in early stages, you have a closing problem. If it's mostly in late stages, you have a prospecting problem.

steps.title: Write Recommendations
steps.description: Create a one-page recommendations document: (1) Top 5 deals to accelerate (and how). (2) Top 5 deals to disqualify or deprioritise (and why). (3) Pipeline health summary with key metrics. (4) Three actions to improve overall pipeline performance.
steps.tips: Be decisive. The most valuable pipeline advice is 'stop wasting time on these 5 deals and focus energy on these 5 instead.' Managers value reps who can self-diagnose.

deliverables: A pipeline spreadsheet with MEDDIC RAG scores for all 25 deals
Sales velocity calculations (overall and by stage)
A pipeline health summary with coverage ratio and stage distribution analysis
A one-page recommendations document with accelerate, disqualify, and improvement actions

rubric.description: MEDDIC scoring is applied consistently with clear evidence for each rating. Sales velocity is calculated correctly and interpreted meaningfully.
Stalled, under-qualified, and at-risk deals are identified with specific reasons. No deal is flagged without a clear rationale.
Recommendations are specific, prioritised, and actionable. Each deal recommendation includes a clear next action, not just a diagnosis.
The analysis could be presented in a pipeline review meeting. It's concise, data-driven, and focuses on the most important takeaways.
